resources.string: Which declarative framework is used to build modern UI in Kotlin?
Jetpack Compose allows building declarative interfaces.
Which annotation marks a composable function?
\@Composable marks functions that draw UI in Compose.
Which function remembers state between recompositions?
Which function creates observable state in Compose?
mutableStateOf creates state that Compose can observe.
Which layout allows elements to overlap?
Box allows placing elements on top of each other.
Which component displays text in Compose?
Which component represents a button in Compose Material?
Which object is used to configure size, padding, click behavior and style of a composable?
Modifier configures composable behavior and appearance.
Which modifier makes a composable take all available size?
Which modifier makes an element clickable?
Which component displays content inside a Material card?
Card groups content with a card style.
Which component is commonly used as a visual Material base container?
Surface applies Material color, shape and elevation.
Which tool manages navigation between screens in Navigation Compose?
Where are composable navigation routes declared?
Which function creates a NavController in Compose?
rememberNavController() creates and remembers the navigation controller.
Which method navigates to a route?
Which class usually manages UI state and logic?
ViewModel keeps UI state and logic.
Which function runs suspend effects in Compose?
LaunchedEffect launches a coroutine associated with Compose.
Which function remembers state and also keeps it through simple recreations?
rememberSaveable keeps state using saveable mechanisms.
What does recomposition mean in Compose?
Running composables again when state changes
Recomposition updates the UI when state changes.
Which file usually declares permissions and main app components?
AndroidManifest.xml declares permissions, activities and configuration.
Which class is usually the visual entry point of an Android app?
MainActivity usually starts the app content.
Which method is normally used in an Activity to place Compose content?
setContent defines the Compose tree of the Activity.
Which file stores translatable texts in Android?
Which folder usually contains text, color and theme resources?
res/values contains resources such as strings, colors and themes.
Which system is commonly used to build Android projects?
Android uses Gradle as its build system.
Which file defines dependencies for an Android module?
build.gradle.kts or build.gradle defines module dependencies.
A theme system for colors, typography and shapes
MaterialTheme applies a Material visual theme.
Which component displays an efficient vertical list?
Which component displays an efficient horizontal list?
Which function allows collecting Flow state in Compose?
collectAsState converts a Flow into observable State.
Which type represents a running coroutine?
Which library is used for coroutines in Kotlin?
Which function allows showing an image from drawable resources?
Which component allows text input in Compose?
What is recommended to separate UI logic?
MVVM separates UI, state and logic.
APK is the installable Android package.
Which modern format distributes apps on Google Play?
Which file usually contains custom colors?
Which resource is used for vector images?
Which annotation is used to launch previews in Android Studio?
\@Preview shows composables in the design panel.
Which problem does observable state avoid in Compose?
The UI not updating when data changes
State hoisting moves state to a higher composable.
Which principle recommends that a composable function should not modify global state without control?
Unidirectional data flow helps keep UI predictable.
Jetpack Compose uses composable functions to build UI.
Compose is based on functions annotated with @Composable.
mutableStateOf allows Compose to observe state changes.
When observed state changes, Compose can recompose.
NavController is used to navigate between screens.
NavController manages navigation in Navigation Compose.
Box can place elements in layers.
Text is the basic text composable.
Button uses onClick to handle taps.
Modifier allows applying padding, size or click behavior.
fillMaxSize() tries to take all available space.
rememberSaveable can keep state through simple recreations.
Recomposition means running affected composables again.
LaunchedEffect can launch a coroutine from Compose.
navigate(\"profile\") works even if the profile route does not exist.
ViewModel helps separate UI state and logic.
ViewModel avoids putting all logic in the screen.
Permissions are declared in the manifest.
MainActivity is usually the visual entry point of the app.
setContent is used to define Compose content in an Activity.
strings.xml is used for translatable texts.
The module Gradle file declares dependencies.
MaterialTheme defines colors, typography and shapes.
LazyColumn is better than Column for long lists.
collectAsState allows observing a Flow in Compose.
collectAsState converts emissions into Compose state.
\@Preview is used to see composables in Android Studio.
painterResource can load images from resources.
MVVM separates model, view and logic/state.
APK is an installable Android package.
AAB is a modern distribution format for Google Play.
Android App Bundle is used for distribution.
INTERNET is declared as a permission to use network access.
A normal state like var count = 0 always recomposes automatically.
It must be observable state to recompose.
State hoisting means lifting state to the parent.
Unidirectional data flow helps make UI predictable.
A composable function should be as predictable as possible.
Predictability makes recomposition and maintenance easier.
Recomposition should be used to run heavy operations directly.
Heavy operations should be avoided during recomposition.
Declarative UI describes what to show according to state.
Compose describes UI based on state.
Changing observed State can trigger recomposition.
setContent allows using Compose in an Activity.
res/values can contain strings.xml and colors.xml.
A route with arguments can be defined in Navigation Compose.
popUpTo can clear part of the back stack.
Composable and ViewModel should be exactly the same thing.
Composable draws UI; ViewModel manages state and logic.
Complete the code:\n\nvar name by remember { ____(\"\") }
Complete the code:\n\nvar count by ____ { mutableStateOf(0) }
Complete the code:\n\nvar text by ____ { mutableStateOf(\"\") }
Complete the code:\n\n____(value = name, onValueChange = { name = it })
Complete the code:\n\n____ { items(list) { item -> Text(item) } }
Complete the code:\n\n____ { items(list) { item -> Text(item) } }
Complete the code:\n\nval state by flow.____()
com.android.application applies the Android app plugin.
Complete the code:\n\nval scale by animateFloatAsState(targetValue = 1f, label = \"____\")
Complete the code:\n\nval color by animateColorAsState(targetValue = Color.Red, label = \"____\")
Process of redrawing UI when state changes
Compose recomposes parts of the UI when state changes.
Object used to configure size, padding, click behavior, etc.
Modifier allows decorating and configuring composables.
Class used to manage UI state and logic
ViewModel separates logic and state from the screen.
Lifting state to the parent composable
State hoisting improves reuse and control.
App configuration file for permissions and components
The manifest declares key information about the app.
Visual theme for colors, typography and shapes
State that tries to survive recreations
To use Text in Compose, the function must be composable.
remember/mutableStateOf should be used so Compose can recompose.

Find the problem:

navController.navigate("profile")

but composable("profile") does not exist
The route must be defined in the NavHost.
remember does not create mutable observable state by itself
To change and observe a value, mutableStateOf would be used.
Text is not inside the Button content
Button content must go inside the braces.
Blocking infinite loop without delay or useful suspension
An infinite loop without suspension can block execution.
It must go in the Manifest
Text needs a String; use stringResource(R.string.app_name).
Find the problem:\n\ncomposable(\"game/{id}\") { val id = it.arguments?.getInt(\"id\") }
If navArgument is not declared, the expected type may fail
composable does not receive a lambda
It is recommended to declare navArgument with its type.
Find the problem:\n\nI make a network request directly in the composable body on every recomposition.
It may run many times; better use an effect or ViewModel
It is mandatory to do it this way
Which component displays text in Compose?
What happens when an observed mutableStateOf changes?
Compose can recompose composables that read that state.
Which function would you use to load a string from resources?
Which function would you use to navigate to profile?
Which component would you use for a long vertical list?
LazyColumn is efficient for long lists.
Which component would you use for text input?
Which indicator would you use for circular loading?
Which file would you edit to add the Internet permission?
Permissions are declared in the Manifest.
Order the basic structure of a Compose screen:
First you mark it composable, declare the function and place UI inside.
Order the basic flow for a counter button:
First create state, then the button and text.
First the controller, then host, routes and navigation.
Order the flow of an efficient list:
LazyColumn contains items and each item draws content.
Order the basic use of a ViewModel:
ViewModel exposes state and receives events.
Order the flow to add a translatable text:
First resource, then reading and display.
Order the flow to use a drawable image:
First resource, then load and use.
Order the flow for a safe request from UI:
The UI recomposes with the new state
The UI should not run heavy logic directly.
Order the flow to define a route with an argument:
Arguments are defined, declared, read and passed.
Order the flow to create a new screen:
Object used to modify size, padding and click behavior.
Class for UI state and logic.
Process of updating UI because state changed.
Clue: annotation for a Compose UI function.
LazyColumn is an efficient vertical list.
